content: Biological and chemical agents from the traditional definition of "agent" can be framed as naturally occurring autonomous entities that evolved a primitive equivalent of the 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism: their intrinsic reward for exploring novel environments is encoded as evolutionary fitness from colonizing unoccupied niches. This re-framing suggests we can design more robust, adaptive ICM-powered AI agents by borrowing evolutionary strategies that shape natural agents' exploratory behaviors.
